news.summary: A recent collaboration has emerged, merging EagleView's accurate aerial data with ServiceTitan's estimating and proposal features. This integration aims to enhance workflows, ultimately leading to improved close rates. By combining EagleView's precise aerial data with ServiceTitan's capabilities, the integration streamlines operations and drives higher close rates for businesses. This collaboration is set to transform the way companies operate by leveraging the strengths of both platforms. With this partnership, businesses can expect more efficient processes and potentially higher success rates in closing deals.
news.headline: ServiceTitan partners with EagleView for aerial data integration to streamline workflows, driving higher close rates.

Additionally, an adjusted EBITDA of $10,001 million was recorded for the quarter, which represents 67 percent of...
news.headline: Broadcom Inc. reports Q2 revenue at $15B, up 20% YOY. GAAP net income $4.9B; Non-GAAP $7.8B. Adjusted EBITDA $10B.

news.summary: lululemon athletica inc. revealed its financial performance for the initial quarter of fiscal 2025, concluding on May 4, 2025. With Calvin McDonald, the Chief Executive Officer, announcing positive results, the company experienced growth in various areas such as channels, categories, and markets, notably in the U.S. This indicates the robust and adaptable nature of the business model. Moreover, customer reception towards product innovations, fresh additions, and brand initiatives was favorable.
news.headline: lululemon athletica inc. (NASDAQ:LULU) reports growth in Q1 fiscal 2025 ended May 4, 2025. CEO Calvin McDonald highlights growth across channels, categories, and markets, including the U.S.

news.summary: In Hanover, Pennsylvania, Utz Brands, Inc. (NYSE: UTZ) disclosed that the company, known for producing branded salty snacks, declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of around $0.061 per share on its Class A Common Stock. The dividend payment, anticipated to be approximately on July 3, 2025, is set to be distributed to stockholders recorded as of June 16, 2025. This announcement came as the company reiterated its position as a small-cap value Staples equity.
news.headline: Utz Brands, Inc. (NYSE: UTZ) declares a quarterly cash dividend of $0.061 per share on Class A Common Stock. Payment on July 3, 2025.

news.summary: In City of Industry, California, Torrid Holdings Inc., a leading direct-to-consumer brand on the NYSE as CURV, catering to women sizes 10 to 30 in North America, shared its financial outcomes for the quarter concluding on May 3, 2025. The company's CEO, Lisa Harper, expressed satisfaction with their advancements in strategic goals during this period. The first-quarter results matched projections, with a reported revenue of $266 million.
news.headline: Torrid reports Q1 2025 results, revenue of $266 million. CEO satisfied with progress.

news.summary: Retail giant Walmart Inc. (NYSE: WMT) shared preliminary voting outcomes from its recent Annual Shareholders’ Meeting in Bentonville, Ark. A notable 91.4% of total outstanding shares were accounted for during the event. Doug McMillon, serving as President and CEO, highlighted Walmart's robust performance during fiscal year 2025. McMillon stressed the company's focus on boosting wages, enhancing opportunities, driving innovation, investing in technology, and contributing to community development. He expressed confidence in Walmart's ability to enhance return on investment in conjunction with its ongoing initiatives.
news.headline: Walmart reports 91.4% share representation at 2025 Annual Shareholders' Meeting with strong fiscal results and CEO's focus on investments and community impact.

news.summary: Constellation has recently confirmed that it has obtained regulatory approval from the Public Utility Commission of Texas for the acquisition of Calpine Corporation, marking a significant milestone in the transaction process. This approval allows Constellation to proceed with the acquisition of Calpine Corporation, a move that is poised to greatly impact the energy sector. With this approval in place, Constellation is now positioned to enhance its presence and further solidify its standing within the industry.
news.headline: Constellation receives regulatory approval from Texas for acquisition of Calpine Corporation.

news.summary: In New York, Braze (Nasdaq: BRZE), a top customer engagement platform, revealed its fiscal quarter results ending April 30, 2025. According to Bill Magnuson, Braze's CEO, the company has had a promising start in fiscal year 2026, witnessing substantial revenue growth, profitability, and free cash flow despite market fluctuations. Additionally, Ed McDonnell is set to join Braze in July to oversee various aspects of the business.
news.headline: Braze reports strong Q1 2026 results: revenue growth, profitability, and free cash flow. Ed McDonnell to join as leader.

news.summary: In Utah's Silicon Slopes, Domo (Nasdaq: DOMO) revealed its recognition as a top contender in Nucleus Research’s 2025 Business Intelligence (BI) and Analytics Technology Value Matrix, marking its fifth consecutive year achieving this status. Domo was commended for its proficiency in integrating cutting-edge analytics, interactive dashboards, and nearly real-time visualizations to convert data into actionable intelligence. Nucleus Research praised Domo's extensive library of over 1,000 pre-built connectors, which aid in consolidating data sources seamlessly.
news.headline: Domo named leader in 2025 Nucleus Research Analytics Technology Value Matrix - 5th consecutive year. Domo has over 1,000 pre-built connectors.

news.summary: Rubrik, Inc. (NYSE: RBRK), a cyber resilience company based in Palo Alto, California, has reported its financial results for the first quarter of fiscal year 2026, which ended on April 30, 2025. The company exceeded all guided metrics for the quarter. Bipul Sinha, Rubrik’s CEO and Chairman, expressed satisfaction with the results, emphasizing the impact of their concentrated innovation and operational efforts. Sinha believes they are leading in the cyber resilience market and sees a significant growth opportunity ahead for the company.
news.headline: Rubrik (NYSE: RBRK) reports Q1 fiscal year 2026 results ended April 30, 2025, with outstanding performance exceeding guided metrics.

news.summary: On June 5, 2025, in Sacramento, California, the state Senate passed a bill for a regional electricity system with bipartisan support. This move is similar to the vote on the initial electricity deregulation bill in 1996. The legislation overturns state protections from the Enron era.
news.headline: CA Senate approves legislation for regional electricity deregulation despite concerns. Bipartisan vote, reminiscent of 1996, GOP Senator praises as "big beautiful bill."

news.headline: Staffmark Group receives Silver Rating from EcoVadis, top 15% globally in sustainability. 130,000+ companies evaluated.

news.headline: Rexford Industrial releases ESG & TCFD reports, focused on investing in industrial properties in SoCal.

news.summary: The 2025 Patriot Awards Gala will honor notable individuals including former Defense Secretary Robert Gates, Fox News Correspondent Johnny "Joey" Jones, Country Singer and Songwriter John Rich, and Philanthropist Kathleen Rollins. The event is scheduled to take place in Chattanooga, Tennessee on June 5th, 2025. This year's honorees represent a diverse range of backgrounds and contributions, showcasing the importance of recognizing excellence across various fields. Stay tuned for more updates on the upcoming gala this fall.
news.headline: 2025 Patriot Awards Gala in Chattanooga to honor Former Defense Secretary Gates, Fox News Correspondent Jones, Country Singer Rich, and Philanthropist Rollins.

This acknowledgment highlights the company's innovative approach to utilizing technology to improve healthcare outcomes.
news.headline: Massive Bio honored for AI-driven clinical trial matching & patient recruitment in oncology by CB Insights.

news.summary: Rent the Runway reported its financial results for the fiscal quarter ending April 30, 2025, showcasing a return to subscriber growth with over 147,000 active subscribers, the highest quarter-ending count in the company's history. The company experienced the strongest quarterly customer retention in four years due to its new inventory strategy, resulting in a significant improvement in subscriber count and customer loyalty. In Q1 2025, Rent the Runway saw a 24% increase in new inventory receipts compared to Q1 2024 and launched 36 brands along with more than 1,000 new styles. Key financial highlights include a revenue of $69.6 million (down 7.2% YoY), gross profit of $21.9 million, and ending total subscribers at 182,209. Adjusted EBITDA was $(1.3) million. Rent the Runway expects revenue between $76-80 million in Q2 2025, with a double-digit growth in ending active subscribers for fiscal year 2025. However, the company cautioned about potential unknowns such as economic changes and tariffs that could impact results. Rent the Runway's financial metrics and outlook emphasize a renewed focus on customer engagement and strategic improvements.
news.headline: Rent the Runway, Inc. announces Q1 2025 results with $69.6M revenue, over 147,000 active subscribers, a 1% increase, and $21.9M gross profit.

news.summary: Dollar Tree Inc. (NASDAQ: DLTR) exceeded expectations with its first-quarter earnings, reporting a 11.3% increase in net sales to $4.6 billion. This surpassed both the consensus of $4.53 billion and the company's own guidance of $4.5 billion to $4.6 billion. Same-store net sales rose by 5.4%, fueled by a 2.5% uptick in traffic and a 2.8% increase in average ticket. This outperformed the company's projected growth of 3% to 5%. Adjusted earnings per share were reported at $1.26, surpassing the analyst estimate of $1.21 and the company's expectation of $1.10 to $1.25. Dollar Tree also reconfirmed its sales guidance for fiscal year 2025 at $18.5 billion to $19.11 billion, compared to the consensus of $18.95 billion, predicated on a comparable store net sales growth range of 3% to 5%. The company's outlook is based on the assumption that current tariff levels will persist for the remainder of the year.
news.headline: Dollar Tree Inc. exceeded Q1 expectations with $4.6 billion in net sales, 11.3% growth, $1.26 EPS, and 5.4% same-store sales increase. FY 2025 guidance is $18.5B—$19.11B. Analysts raise forecasts.

news.summary: ServiceTitan, a software platform for trades, reported financial results for the fiscal first quarter ended April 30, 2025. The company saw a 22% year-over-year growth in gross transaction volume (GTV), reaching $17.7 billion. Total revenue for the quarter was $215.7 million, up 27% from the previous year. The platform revenue also showed a 27% increase, totaling $208 million. Non-GAAP income from operations reached $16.2 million, showing significant improvement. The company expects total revenue for the fiscal second quarter 2026 to be between $228-230 million and between $910-920 million for the full fiscal year. ServiceTitan is making significant strides in expanding its offerings and improving operational efficiency.
news.headline: ServiceTitan announced its fiscal first quarter results for 2025, with $17.7B in GTV (22% YoY growth) and $215.7M in total revenue (27% YoY growth). Non-GAAP income from operations was $16.2M with a 7.5% operating margin. The company expects total revenue of $228-230M for Q2 2026 and $910-920M for the full year, positioning themselves for transformative customer outcomes. Operating expenses totaled $197.9M, resulting in a loss of $49.5M. For more detailed financial information, visit the original press release.

news.summary: Concrete Pumping Holdings, Inc. reported financial results for the second quarter ended April 30, 2025. The company's revenue was $94.0 million compared to $107.1 million the previous year, with a gross profit of $36.2 million and income from operations of $8.3 million. Net loss was $0.0 million compared to a net income of $3.0 million. Debt outstanding was $425.0 million with net debt of $387.2 million and total liquidity of $352.5 million. Adjusted EBITDA was $22.5 million. The company's U.S. Concrete Pumping segment revenue decreased to $62.1 million, while U.S. Concrete Waste Management revenue increased to $18.1 million. The company expects fiscal year 2025 revenue between $380.0 million to $390.0 million. A new $15.0 million share repurchase program was approved. The company is focused on generating free cash flow, deleveraging the balance sheet, and pursuing strategic mergers and acquisitions. The company aims to position itself for growth in fiscal year 2026. Forward-looking statements and non-GAAP financial measures were also discussed in the article. For more detailed financial statements and information, visit Concrete Pumping Holdings' official website or refer to the original article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Concrete Pumping Holdings reported Q2 earnings, with $94.0M revenue, $36.2M gross profit, $8.3M income from operations, and $0.4M net loss. Adjusted EBITDA was $22.5M, with $352.5M liquidity and a $15M share repurchase program approved. CEO focuses on strategic pricing and future value creation amid challenging construction environment. Revenue breakdown includes $62.1M in U.S. Concrete Pumping, $18.1M in U.S. Concrete Waste Management, and $13.8M in U.K. Operations. Fiscal year 2025 outlook expects revenue of $380.0M to $390.0M, Adjusted EBITDA of $95.0M to $100.0M, and free cash flow of $45.0M. Financial measures and segment results detailed. Leverage ratio at 3.7x with debt at $425.0M and net debt at $387.2M. Share repurchase program extended by $15.0M. CEO affirms focus on shareholder value, operational execution, and growth opportunities. Company to hold conference call on Q2 results. Non-GAAP financial measures provided for evaluation. Total assets at $878.79M and total liabilities at $591.95M. Full details on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: Oil-Dri Corporation of America, a producer of sorbent mineral products, announced its third-quarter and nine-month results for fiscal year 2025. In the third quarter, net sales reached $115.5 million, a 8% increase from the previous year, with a consolidated gross profit of $33.0 million. Operating income for the quarter was $13.9 million, a 33% increase from the prior year. Net income rose by 50% to $11.6 million. The Business to Business segment saw a revenue increase of 18%, while the Retail and Wholesale segment had a 3% increase in net sales. Oil-Dri's EBITDA grew by 35% to $20.2 million. The company's cash and cash equivalents reached $36.5 million as of April 30, 2025. Consolidated net sales have seen growth for 16 consecutive quarters. The earnings discussion for the quarter is scheduled for June 6, 2025. Oil-Dri continues to focus on strategic growth and customer satisfaction.
news.headline: Oil-Dri Corp of America reports highest Q3 results: Net Sales at $115.5M, Operating Income at $13.9M, Net Income at $11.6M, EBITDA at $20.2M.

news.summary: In Broomfield, Colorado on June 5, 2025, Vail Resorts, Inc. (NYSE: MTN) released their third-quarter fiscal 2025 results, covering the period ending on April 30, 2025. The report included updated guidance for fiscal 2025 and disclosed the early season pass sales results. Notably, the company recorded net income attributable to Vail Resorts during this period.
news.headline: Vail Resorts reports Q3 results for fiscal 2025 and early season pass sales; NYSE: MTN; ended April 30, 2025.

news.summary: On June 5, 2025, Docusign, Inc. (NASDAQ: DOCU) shared its fiscal quarter performance till April 30, 2025. The company will make available prepared remarks and a news release containing the financial results on its website at investor.docusign.com before the event.
news.headline: DocuSign reports Q1 fiscal 2026 results; $1.0B added to share repurchase program. Website will have full details.

news.summary: Petco Health and Wellness Company, Inc. (Nasdaq: WOOF) has confirmed its fiscal 2025 net sales and earnings outlook. In their latest financial report for the first quarter of 2025, the company revealed that net sales totaled $1.5 billion, showing a 2.3% decline compared to the previous year. This announcement was made in San Diego on June 5, 2025.
news.headline: Petco reports Q1 2025 net sales of $1.5 billion, down 2.3% year over year, reaffirms fiscal 2025 outlook.

news.summary: Experienced automotive professional Douglas Grimm has been appointed to the Board of Directors of Lucid Group, Inc., a leading manufacturer of cutting-edge electric vehicles. The announcement was made on June 5, 2025, in Newark, California. Lucid Group is known for producing the most advanced electric vehicles globally and is listed on the NASDAQ stock exchange under the ticker symbol LCID. Grimm brings a wealth of industry knowledge to the board, enhancing the company's strategic leadership.
news.headline: Lucid Group elects Douglas Grimm to Board of Directors, bringing extensive industry experience. Date: June 5, 2025.

news.summary: In Tokyo, BGO has successfully concluded the fundraising for its flagship real estate strategy, BentallGreenOak Asia IV (BGO Asia Fund IV), with a total of over US$5.1 billion secured in capital commitments for the Fund and co-investment sidecars. This marks the highest closed-end capital raise in BGO's history, highlighting its position as a leading global real estate investment manager in Asian markets. The Fund is aimed at targeting valuable opportunities in the region.
news.headline: BGO raises $5.1 billion for Asia real estate, its largest fundraise ever. Firm showcases strength in global market.

news.summary: Barings, a leading real estate investment manager, has recently granted a loan of $548.5 million to refinance a collection of nine multifamily properties comprising over 2,500 units across six different states. These states include Arizona, Colorado, Missouri, Minnesota, Michigan, and Florida. The properties are under the ownership of Thompson Thrift, a prominent real estate development company. These assets were completed and delivered by Thompson Thrift between the first quarter of 2024 and the second quarter of 2025.
news.headline: $548.5M loan from Barings refinances 9 multifamily communities with 2,500 units in 6 states owned by Thompson Thrift.

news.summary: In a recent statement, RH (NYSE: RH) from CORTE MADERA, Calif., disclosed that its financial results for the first quarter ending on May 3, 2025, will be released after market close on Thursday, June 12, 2025. The update will feature a shareholder letter from RH's Chairman and CEO, Gary Friedman, discussing the Company's progress and recent performance. Interested stakeholders can access the shareholder letter along with the comprehensive financial results on the Company's platform.

news.summary: The company experienced an impressive 41% increase in growth compared to the previous year, thanks to the successful introduction of their compact launcher and expansion into retail markets.
news.headline: Byrna Technologies reports record revenues of $28.5 million in fiscal second quarter, propelled by 41% year-over-year growth.
